title: Repeated full-corpus loading of event ledger into memory
description: readEventLedger reads and JSON-parses the entire events.jsonl file into memory. It is called repeatedly (8+ times) across inspection functions like inspectEventLedger, inspectCiteGoodhart, buildDriftedStableIds, and inspectDriftUnconsumed. For large event ledgers this results in unbounded memory allocation and CPU overhead.
fix_direction: Parse the ledger once per doctor run and pass the resulting events array to all dependent inspection functions, or use a stream parser.

title: Synchronous file system reads in full-corpus scans (iterateCanonicalEntries)
description: iterateCanonicalEntries uses readdirSync and readFileSync. It is called multiple times sequentially across inspections (inspectDraftAutoPromote, inspectDraftBacklog, inspectKnowledgeTagsEmpty). This causes N+1 blocking sync I/O calls scaling linearly with knowledge entries, blocking the event loop on hot paths.
fix_direction: Replace sync FS calls with async variants (fs/promises) or cache the canonical entries map across the doctor run.

title: Sequential asynchronous file system writes during lint application
description: In runDoctorApplyLint, file mutations (applyOrphanDemote, applyStaleArchive, applyPendingAutoArchive) are performed sequentially in for...of loops instead of concurrently. This linearly increases execution time on large repositories with many auto-fixable findings.
fix_direction: Use Promise.all to process independent file mutations concurrently within each lint pass, or batch the I/O operations.

title: Synchronous I/O in hook inspections
description: inspectHooksRuntime, inspectHooksContentDrift, and inspectHooksWired use synchronous methods (readFileSync, readdirSync, statSync) to inspect hook files, blocking the event loop.
fix_direction: Refactor hook inspection functions to use async fs/promises and evaluate them concurrently within the initial Promise.all block.
